questions.question: A right triangle has an angle of 35° and the side opposite to it measures 7 cm. What is the length of the hypotenuse? Round to the nearest tenth.
questions.explanation: Use the sine ratio: sin(35°) = 7/h, so h = 7/sin(35°) ≈ 7/0.574 ≈ 12.2 cm.

questions.question: If cos(θ) = 3/5 and θ is in the first quadrant, what is sin(θ)?
questions.explanation: Use the Pythagorean identity: sin²(θ) + cos²(θ) = 1. So sin²(θ) = 1 − 9/25 = 16/25, giving sin(θ) = 4/5 in the first quadrant.

questions.question: A ladder leans against a wall, forming a 62° angle with the ground. If the ladder is 15 feet long, how high does it reach on the wall?
questions.explanation: The height is the side opposite the 62° angle. Use sin(62°) = h/15, so h = 15·sin(62°) ≈ 15(0.883) ≈ 13.2 feet.

questions.question: Simplify: sin²(x) + sin²(x)·cot²(x)
questions.explanation: Factor out sin²(x): sin²(x)(1 + cot²(x)). Since 1 + cot²(x) = csc²(x) = 1/sin²(x), the product is sin²(x)·(1/sin²(x)) = 1.

questions.question: On the unit circle, a point is located at angle 240°. What are its coordinates?
questions.explanation: 240° = 180° + 60°, placing the point in the third quadrant. Use reference angle 60°: x = −cos(60°) = −1/2, y = −sin(60°) = −√3/2.

questions.question: A surveyor stands 50 meters from the base of a building and measures the angle of elevation to the top as 48°. What is the height of the building? Round to the nearest whole number.
